target: data/predictions.json — 12 entries with restates_win set but our_verdict != recycled
methodology: For each prediction: (1) compare claim text to referenced WIN claim, (2) check whether any independently testable content is added beyond the WIN, (3) if pure restatement → recommend recycled; if independent content exists → keep current verdict and document reasoning.

entries.reasoning: PRED-095 refines WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic ensemble, Self-Contradicted) by adding specific testable thresholds: amplitude −17 to −21 nT and >80% station coverage. Crucially, it is genuinely prospective (test window: August 2026 eclipse). The specific thresholds create an independent test — WIN-068's verdict covers the general claim but this prediction will generate a concrete pass/fail result when the eclipse occurs. Keeping pending until the test window closes is correct. When the window closes, the verdict should reflect whether the specific thresholds were met, with a note that even if they are, WIN-068's Self-Contradicted verdict still applies to the underlying framework.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Restates WIN-068 core claim but adds specific amplitude (−17 to −21 nT) and coverage (>80% stations) thresholds not in the WIN. Genuinely prospective — retains independent test value. Verdict deferred to test window closure (Aug 2026).

entries.reasoning: PRED-R002 (SAA African cell ≤21,750 nT by 2028) is essentially identical to WIN-035 (SAA African < 21,795 nT, Std Model Explains). The 45 nT threshold difference is negligible — both predict continued SAA weakening, which WMM2025 and IGRF-14 already project. The detail_reasoning itself states "WIN-035 already covers this exact claim." No independent test is introduced; the 2028 timeline merely extends the observation window for the same phenomenon. The standard_physics verdict is already captured by WIN-035's Std Model Explains verdict.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: essentially identical to WIN-035 (45 nT threshold difference negligible). SAA weakening already projected by WMM2025/IGRF-14.

entries.reasoning: PRED-CURR-004 ("Southern hemisphere inflow toward SAA") is a vaguer restatement of WIN-024 (Roaring 40s = SAA boundary, Misleading). The prediction adds no measurable criteria — no station, no amplitude, no frequency, no test window. It is strictly less specific than WIN-024, which at least named the Roaring 40s. The "unfalsifiable" verdict merely reflects the restatement's vagueness, not any independent content. WIN-024's Misleading verdict already covers the substance.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: vague restatement of WIN-024 (SAA atmospheric flow) with no additional measurable criteria.

entries.reasoning: PRED-CURR-008 ("Single global eastward circulation rotation") restates WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE, Misleading). The "single global eastward circulation" is the jet stream / Coriolis effect — standard physics already covered in WIN-058's assessment. No new test criteria, data source, or measurement threshold is introduced.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: restates WIN-058's atmospheric circulation claim. Jet stream = standard Coriolis physics, already assessed in WIN-058.

entries.reasoning: PRED-V13-004 ("Aetheric slipstream routes correlate with great circles") restates WIN-058's flight route claim. Great-circle routes are geodesics on the WGS84 ellipsoid that airlines use for fuel efficiency — this is the definition of shortest-path navigation on a sphere. The dome's "aetheric slipstream" is a relabeling of great-circle routing. WIN-058 already covers this.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: great-circle routes = geodesics on WGS84 ellipsoid, already covered in WIN-058. "Aetheric slipstream" relabels standard navigation.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TESLA-002 claims "Tesla/Schumann frequency ratio approaches 1.574" — but 1.574 is the dome's own locked aetheric velocity constant VA = 1.574c (hardcoded in monitor.py). This is circular by construction: the ratio is defined to equal the model's own parameter. No independent test exists because the "prediction" is a tautology derived from WIN-001's Tesla resonance framework. The circularity makes this a restatement, not an independent claim.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: 1.574 is the dome's own hardcoded VA constant. The ratio "prediction" is circular by construction from WIN-001 parameters.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TESLA-004 claims "Tesla harmonic series exhibits ovoid splitting patterns" — but "ovoid splitting" has no operational definition in spectral analysis, acoustics, or electromagnetic theory. Without a measurable quantity, this is not a prediction but a label applied to WIN-002's Schumann harmonic claim using dome-specific jargon. The undefined terminology prevents any independent test.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: "ovoid splitting" has no operational definition. Restates WIN-002 Schumann claim with undefined dome jargon.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TOROID-002 claims "Two-pole toroidal wavelength λ_g = 8,619 km" — but 8,619 km is the dome's own exponential decay constant from the firmament height function H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619). This is not an independently measured wavelength; it is the dome's own parameter relabeled. The "wavelength" is WIN-053's decay constant repackaged. No independent measurement or test is proposed.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: 8,619 km is the dome's own exponential decay constant from WIN-053, relabeled as a "wavelength." No independent test.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TOROID-003 claims Schumann mode splitting Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz. While this is a specific number, it is derived from dome model parameters (not from measured Schumann spectra) and has no observational basis. Real Schumann mode splitting is caused by day-night ionospheric asymmetry and varies continuously — it is not a fixed constant. The 0.336 Hz value is a dome construction, not a testable prediction. WIN-038 (Schumann 7.83 Hz stable, Self-Contradicted) already covers the dome's Schumann framework. ISS-826 also flagged this as unclassified.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: 0.336 Hz splitting derived from dome parameters, not observed spectra. Real Schumann splitting varies continuously (day-night asymmetry). WIN-038 covers the framework.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TOROID-004 (Antarctic magnetometer κ enhancement from toroidal field) attributes Antarctic magnetic anomalies to the dome's "toroidal field." Antarctic magnetometer anomalies are well-documented consequences of Earth's field geometry and SAA dynamics, already covered in WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model, Self-Contradicted). The dome relabels standard geomagnetic phenomena using "toroidal" terminology. No new test criteria are introduced.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: Antarctic magnetic anomalies are standard SAA/geomagnetic effects. Restates WIN-053 with "toroidal" relabeling.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TOROID-005 claims "sub-terrestrial toroidal current peak at 40–60° latitude" — positing currents through a "sub-terrestrial sump" beneath the dome. This is an internal component of WIN-053's two-pole geomagnetic model framework. The "sump" is unobservable, the current is unmeasurable, and the latitude range is too broad to constitute a test. This is a sub-claim of WIN-053 with no independent testability.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: sub-claim of WIN-053's framework. "Sub-terrestrial sump" currents are unobservable; latitude range too broad for independent test.

entries.reasoning: PRED-TOROID-007 claims "south polar field excess scales with λ_g = 8,619 km" — identical circularity to PRED-TOROID-002. The 8,619 km parameter is the dome's own exponential decay constant from WIN-053. Claiming that a phenomenon "scales with" the model's own parameter is a tautology, not a prediction.
entries.recommended_notes_update: Recycled: circular — 8,619 km is WIN-053's own decay constant. Claiming field excess "scales with" the model's parameter is tautological.

kept_entries: PRED-095 (pending — genuinely prospective with specific test thresholds, Aug 2026 window)
summary.pattern_note: The 11 recycled entries fall into three categories: (1) SAA/geomagnetic restatements of WIN-053/035/024 — 6 entries that relabel standard geophysics using dome terminology (toroidal, sump, λ_g); (2) Atmospheric circulation restatements of WIN-058 — 2 entries renaming jet streams/great circles as "aetheric slipstreams"; (3) Schumann/Tesla restatements of WIN-001/002/038 — 3 entries using dome's hardcoded constants (VA = 1.574c, 8619 km decay) to create circular "predictions." The common thread: the dome generates predictions by repackaging its own parameters and labeling standard physics with proprietary terminology. This inflates the prediction count without adding testable content.

decider_instructions: For the 11 entries recommended for recycled: (1) set our_verdict to "recycled", (2) update the notes field with the recommended_notes_update text, (3) keep the existing detail_reasoning (it documents the analysis that was done). For PRED-095: update notes field only, keep verdict as pending. All changes are to data/predictions.json.
kernel_of_truth: The dome model's prediction generation strategy — deriving specific numerical claims from internal parameters — is a legitimate modeling technique in principle. Real physics does predict specific numbers from theory (e.g., the electron g-factor from QED). The problem is not that the dome derives predictions from its model; the problem is that (a) the model's parameters were fitted to globe data in the first place, making derived predictions circular, and (b) many "predictions" use undefined dome-specific terminology ("ovoid splitting", "aetheric slipstream") that prevents independent verification. A model that derived genuine surprises from its geometry would be worth taking seriously — this one repackages known results.
